Name: Gini
Source (Paper): Gini, Corrado W. "Variability and mutability, contribution to the study of statistical distributions and relations." Studi Economico-Giuridici della R. Universita de Cagliari (1912).

Free Comments: There is a paper publishing the original formula again https://doi.org/10.1007/s10888-011-9188-x, besides there are several definitions in the surveys and benchmarks mentioned. The definitions are all similar to the one provided in the "Pattern Classification" book of Duda, the only exception is the Bommert Benchmark, there is another definition. When excluding Bommert, we still have 5 papers with a similar or no definition, so we can use Gini as defined by Duda,  https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-540-35488-8_4 or https://doi.org/10.1016/j.eswa.2006.04.001
